summary: Large warning-cleanup/header pass aimed at eliminating remaining `implicit-function-declaration` warnings, explicitly described as finishing the rest of `ft` and `gm`. The PR added and corrected many function prototypes across Dolphin, sysdolphin baselib, fighter, game-mode, item, stage, library, and UI headers; added required includes so types were visible at declaration sites; corrected several function signatures from empty-parameter or unknown-return forms to typed prototypes; and adjusted a few C definitions to match those prototypes. Review discussion focused on macro style: do not parenthesize simple literal macro values such as `#define MAX_FT_PARTS 140`.
what_changed: Added many missing prototypes to headers to silence implicit-function-declaration warnings across `ft`, `gm`, Dolphin, baselib, item, stage, library, and related subsystems.
The PR body states the pass 'Finishes the rest of `ft` and `gm`', indicating the cleanup targeted remaining fighter and game-mode warning sites.
Introduced shared type declarations needed by corrected prototypes, including `S32Pair` in `include/dolphin/mtx/mtxtypes.h` and `Event` in `include/dolphin/types.h`.

Corrected existing prototypes from untyped/empty-argument forms to explicit typed signatures, for example `func_80067624(HSD_GObj*, unk_t*)`, Crazy Hand and Master Hand callbacks taking `HSD_GObj*`, Kirby helpers taking `HSD_GObj*` or `Fighter*`, and `GXSetMisc(s32, u32)`.
Added public SDK/system initialization prototypes such as `VIInit`, `CARDInit`, `OSInit`, `PADSetSpec`, and `PADInit`.
Updated `HSD_ObjAllocInit` to take `size_t size` instead of `u32 size`, and changed a fighter initialization call from an oversized constant `0x0000800000008000` to `0x8000`.
In `GXMisc.c`, included its own header and `GXGeometry.h`, changed no-argument definitions to `(void)`, and added parentheses around bitwise expressions to make operator precedence explicit.
In `GXMisc.h`, simplified public prototypes by removing address comments and `extern`, and fixed `GXSetMisc`'s second parameter type to `u32`.

Adjusted assembly-backed declarations in `src/melee/ft/code_80081B38.c` and `.h`, including `func_800849EC(Fighter*, Fighter*)`, `func_80084A18(HSD_GObj*)`, and `func_80081C88(HSD_GObj* dst_gobj, f32 scl_y)`.
Expanded `fighter.c` includes substantially so newly referenced prototypes and types came from headers rather than implicit declarations.
smart_moves: Preferred adding or correcting declarations in subsystem headers rather than relying on local forward declarations, making prototypes reusable across future decomp work.
Made type dependencies explicit at header boundaries, e.g. using `HSD_GObj*`, `Fighter*`, `Vec3*`, `S32Pair*`, `size_t`, and `Event` instead of leaving calls unprototyped.
Used the warning cleanup as an opportunity to correct real API mismatches, such as `HSD_ObjAllocInit` using `size_t` and `GXSetMisc` taking `u32` for its second argument.
Included implementation files' own headers, as in `GXMisc.c`, which helps catch declaration/definition drift.
Converted old-style empty parameter lists like `void GXSetDrawDone()` to strict `void GXSetDrawDone(void)`, preventing accidental unprototyped calls in C.
Kept ambiguous or still-bad areas marked with comments rather than pretending the headers were fully solved, for example the `fighter.c` note about `incompatible-pointer-types because bad headers`.
decomp_lessons: When fixing implicit declaration warnings, the durable fix is usually a correct prototype in the owning header plus the right type includes, not an ad hoc local declaration.
Header cleanup often exposes missing type definitions; centralize reusable simple types such as `S32Pair` and callback aliases like `Event` when multiple declarations need them.
For C, distinguish `func()` from `func(void)`: use `(void)` for functions that take no arguments so the compiler can type-check calls.
Assembly-backed functions still need accurate C signatures in headers and asm function annotations, otherwise later C call sites will keep accumulating casts or warnings.
Including a C file's corresponding header in the C file is useful for validating that the public declaration matches the implementation.

Changing prototypes may force include dependency cleanup; headers should include the minimal type-providing headers they require, such as `dolphin/types.h`, `sysdolphin/baselib/gobj.h`, or fighter headers.
Warning-fix PRs can safely improve expression clarity when the expression is warning-adjacent, as shown by parenthesizing GX bitmask operations to preserve intended precedence.
Do not overfit constants to satisfy a mismatched type. The `HSD_ObjAllocInit` change suggests the previous enormous size literal was a symptom of a bad prototype/type understanding.
naming_conventions: Preserved existing address-style placeholder names such as `func_80084A18`, `func_80375934`, and `lbl_803762C4` when no semantic name was evidenced.
Used subsystem-prefixed known names where already established, such as `Fighter_NewSpawn_80068E40`, `HSD_ObjAllocInit`, `GXSetDrawDone`, `VIInit`, `CARDInit`, `OSInit`, and `PADInit`.
Header guards follow project path-style uppercase names such as `MELEE_EF_EFASYNC_H` and `DOLPHIN_GX_GXMISC_H` in the excerpt.

Review feedback established a macro style preference: simple literal macros should be written without unnecessary parentheses, e.g. `#define MAX_FT_PARTS 140` rather than `#define MAX_FT_PARTS (140)`.
Parameter names may be adjusted to avoid ambiguity or match known meaning, as shown by renaming `scale_y` to `scl_y` for `func_80081C88`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Updated asm function declarations to more accurate return and parameter types without necessarily rewriting the asm bodies, reducing call-site warnings while preserving matching code.
Changed `func_800849EC` from `asm unk_t func_800849EC()` to `asm void func_800849EC(Fighter*, Fighter*)` and `func_80084A18` from `asm unk_t func_80084A18()` to `asm BOOL func_80084A18(HSD_GObj*)`.
Adjusted matrix typing in fighter transformation code from `Mtx*` usage toward `Mtx`, with calls like `HSD_MtxInverse(jobj->mtx, mtx)`, reflecting how array typedefs should be passed.
Parenthesized GX bitwise register expressions to make precedence explicit while preserving the intended low-level register writes.
Evidence is primarily from diff excerpts; no reviewer discussion about matching strategy beyond the macro nit was provided.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.comment: Suggested `#define MAX_FT_PARTS 140`; noted that parentheses are not necessary for literal macro values.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.comment: Asked whether removing parentheses from literal macros had adverse edge cases.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.comment: Explained that macro parentheses are only necessary when expanding an argument in a function-like macro or when expanding to multiple tokens.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.comment: Accepted the style direction and said they would start phasing out parentheses for literals, without doing a bulk cleanup.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that continued fixing `implicit-function-declaration` warnings after pass 6.
Search for remaining `#define NAME (literal)` macros to see where literal macro parentheses were phased out.
Audit `fighter.c` comments mentioning `incompatible-pointer-types because bad headers` to identify remaining bad prototypes.
Search for all declarations of `HSD_ObjAllocInit` to confirm the `size_t` signature propagated consistently.
Find later semantic renames for placeholder functions added here, especially `func_80375934`, `func_8037598C`, `func_80084A18`, and `func_800849EC`.
